transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Yeah, I'm doing really well.
Been busy, busy, but so is everybody, right?
Matt, what are you doing?
How's it going?
Yeah, it's Dandy.
How are you?
It's a whirlwind.
It's good.
The back might be a minute or two late.
We're coming from the same call.
But yeah, excited to catch up.
How's your day going?
Good.
Yeah, lots in the works, obviously, as we're still doing Q4 planning, and then we have to do H1 planning for next year.
So getting a move on all that this week.
And then interviewing for who's going to come in for Luke's role.
That's kind of exciting.
I wasn't sure.
I wanted to ask you about if you're open to share.
earlier this morning.
We are trying to let the market decide, like for us, you know, who's the right fit, whether we actually need or want somebody in the role.
So yeah, still all of that is kind of TBD.
Yeah, it's a lot going on at once.
So best of luck on all that, Victoria.
Hi.
How are you doing?
Good, how are you?
Good.
I was saying, Vivek and I were just in a call, he'll probably be a little bit late, just wrapping that up.

But I figured for today, maybe we could close up or just like have a short discussion around Cashflow Desk, and then move into maybe, to the extent that you know, priorities for the next few months, and also just general performance.
And then I could be outdated here, but I think know,
There are some vendor pages that are ready for your review in Eartable, but maybe I'm out of date on that as well.
Yeah, so we put a hold on the vendor pages until we launched the new design, which should be in about two weeks.
Cool.
Okay, great.
And are you able to share any input on the new design and like how you guys are thinking about the future of vendor pages?
Just asking out of curiosity, I'm not sure if you're able to share.
Yeah, so it's not a lot more than what I've shared.
It's like just more data resources, like more data points and not having as much content as we had before, just because this would be also used.
For, like they would be shared more publicly, so on socials and different channels.
So we would have just a summary section, putting the data points into context.
So like five bullet points.
So yeah, we're, we're testing this new design and then deciding how to, to move forward.
If we get back to the existing design or if we use the new one, but the child pages are still going to be the same.
Okay.
That's very helpful.
And then once you have decided kind of like the future of this is, do you anticipate that there might still be a use case for GrowthX to support that?
Or am I understanding that correctly?
Or let me know if I'm not.
I'm not sure.
I'm

How this will play out, like how if we're going to have this as more of like not going for a big volume and then just going for a few vendors and then using it more as a data product versus an AEO play.
So yeah, I'm not sure yet how it will play out.
Like if we end up going to the original design, like how the pages are now, yeah, we would definitely love to continue having you guys support there.
Okay, great.

Also, let us know too if you want, it sounds like not, but if you would like us to be involved in any capacity, I know it's probably driven by like the tests you're doing with the data scientists that you guys hired, but if there is any way that we can.
Support, and it's just like sharing a little bit of intel on kind of what that next stage looks like.
We can also help provide like ideas for how we could be most useful to you and then let you kind of decide from there.
Anyway, okay.
Thanks for sharing.
I was just kind of curious.
Yeah, of course.
Awesome.
And I know we just, you just missed.
just for more context there.
And it's like, I also didn't know how this was, this was going to play out because I know you've asked like for how long we've known about this and, and all of that.
It's, it's something that came from a different department and they're testing based on the new data points we have.
So it's not something that I, that I've decided to like shape this way.
It's, it's just how things like we got more resources.
So based on these resources, we are deciding where to go.
So yeah, just for some context on where this came from and the motivation.
Thank you.
That is helpful.
I mostly just wanted to, it's good feedback for us of like, we probably should have checked in earlier.
And I just wanted to understand like, A, we should have done that anyway.
But B, had we done that, like, you know, would we have been able to have this conversation a little bit earlier?
So that is helpful.
For us to know.
And I really appreciate you, just like the transparency.
Okay, cool.
I feel like we can chat about the Cashflow Desk.
And then we can, if that changes and there's a little bit more information around like, the version of it you want to scale, maybe then we can have a conversation about how we can support.
And then also, of course, if there are other initiatives, would love to collaborate on those.
But I know Cashflow Desk is top of mind.
So, I know you had just shared thoughts.
had thoughts.
know just
Do you want to just like briefly recap how you guys are thinking about it?
Maybe any feedback from George and we can go from there.
Yeah, so I appreciate you guys' flexibility and like also explaining the details and the thought process from your end.
So long story short, our concern is that we're like on this proposed structure, we're still not going to be able to validate before we have to make a commitment.
And at this point, we don't have clarity on what are other projects we could substitute the cash flow desk project with.
So it's a bit hard for us to commit to a year long of capacity.
If we don't have that clarity.
So I think one way we could potentially solve this is starting the validation period.
So the eight weeks at the point that we start to publish, that would give us some time to see if the strategy is working.
Yeah.
Do you have any thoughts there?
Yeah.
I'll probably end up, Tyler, I'll probably have to loop you in to understand, like, what we can and can't commit to.
I don't want to, like, get out over my skis.

But just an initial question also would be, is there a version of this where maybe we could, I think our priority is just we really want to continue, and I totally understand, the RAMP.com work to whatever degree.
As possible and are willing to be very flexible around what that looks like.
So I would be curious if there is a way to structure this where that portion of the contract maybe would have the, you like 30-day opt-out, but maybe it's significantly smaller in scope.
And then the experimental side, I don't know if there's any flexibility there, but Tyler, I'll let you jump in.
But I'm just curious if there's any way we could structure it where there's some type of commitment for annual work and that can look whatever way.
But yeah, those are just my initial thoughts.
And Tyler, I'll let you share from the sales side of things.
Yeah, I guess our perspective, Victoria, is if we've done a lot of great work together to date, what's the concern about us being able to continue doing great work for the cash flow desk?
Or maybe like...
More specifically, what do you need to validate to know, like, what do we need to accomplish to know that you want to continue that work with us, right?
Like, we don't really know what would cause you guys to opt out.
I think that's the confusion.
Yeah, so I'm glad you bring this up because this is really what, like, I don't want this to sound like it's not that we don't trust you guys.
We know that you can deliver.
It's about the strategy.
Like, this is a bet that's coming from me.

I think that this will be successful, but we don't have any data to look at and prove, like, points that will say, okay, starting as a site from scratch and publishing 10 articles a week mentioning RAMP will get us citations and will increase RAMP's visibility.
This is a, this is a high.
Hypothesis.
I don't know if this is true or not, but I want to test it.
So the two-month period is what will allow us, like through you guys, I'm giving this to you guys, to help to prove that my hypothesis is true.
So that's where my thought comes from.
It's not about if you can implement it or not.
It's about the strategy.
That makes total sense.
And I think we're, it's funny because it's like two sides of the coin.
Like, I think for us, that's the same concern is like, this is a bet and we have full faith that we would be able to do excellent work.
And like, but working on a hypothesis, like, that's why I'm curious if there is a version of this where like, we're very flexible on what it looks like, but there is some type of annual commitment.
It doesn't need to be centered.

Around the cashflow desk, knowing that that is super novel, but is there any room for a version of this where there is more of a long-term commitment and then that aspect, like I want the long-term commitment to be centered around work that we know will work well and is less of a big bet, just to assuage that concern.
And we would get that, like, stability and allow us to plan for resourcing.
And then the cashflow desk, if it doesn't perform in the way that would bear out that hypothesis, then we can pivot.
Would that be possible?
Like, are you open to that?
Who would we need buy-in from for continuing work on RAMP.com?
Well, it's just that at this moment, I don't have any scope.
That's the problem.
I've been the main person.

Jason, on our team, relying on the support, and it was mostly for the vendor pages, and not because of GrowthX performance, because of a shift on the strategy, we're not going to need the same support that we were needing before, so I don't have anything at the moment that I would need the support.
So that's where it's coming from, and yeah, that's why it's hard for me to commit to the year-long capacity now.
What about, oh sorry, Mara, do you mind if I jump in?
Sure, go ahead.
Yeah, I guess, you know, RAMP is definitely a big company.
Are there any colleagues or teams that you think could benefit from some of the work that we do?
We understand that the scope that your team, right, would need us for is the cash flow desk, but...
You know, is there maybe any other folks you think would be worthwhile to introduce us to?
I can't think of, like, because, Matt, do you have any thoughts here?
I think the thinking from our side is that, you know, we're willing to add the scope for RAMP.com at no additional cost.

Since we've created, right, so much content together that's been successful, we know there's something that we can do and have left that open-ended where, you know, maybe it's even, like, creating tools or calculators on the site for you guys.
But I think, you know, if we don't define what the expectations look like for the cash flow desk, it's hard for us to have a 30-day opt-out at any time because we don't know what.
Um, KPIs or performance we need to hit, it's very ambiguous, right?
And so we can't march, like, we can't march towards a goal currently.
It's just, um, we can opt out at any time and we want to increase citations, but we don't know what that looks like specifically.
The 30-day opt-out, I get it.
It's like, um, it's tight.
So that's out of, I understand that's not gonna, um, work for you guys.
So moved on from that and now the ask is the, that we start tracking after we start publishing.
So we start the two month period after we start publishing instead of, um, when we start building.
But, um, so I guess.
Yeah.
So it's, if I'm hearing correctly, it sounds like, um, fully understand there is no benchmark for you all to hit.
And so it becomes extremely ambiguous.

What we define as success, whether to continue the partnership, I might have some ideas for how we could set some sort of benchmark, like some sort of goal, if we want to structure it around like some incentive that like we hit this mark, yes, it automatically converts to annual commitment.
Like I can look into that if that's one of the main sticking points here.
I'm thinking, Victoria, if we could potentially like get some data from Josh on new facts and see how quickly that has gained traction because they basically spun that up from nothing.
And so that at least kind of gives us some sort of benchmark for, you know, what we might expect from this project.
We'd have to account for volume, but.
Yeah, I think that could be a good benchmark.
Okay.
And Victoria, guess, help me make sure I understand what you're saying in terms of like the two months out.
I don't fully understand what you're saying there, so just help me understand that.
Yeah, so I think the initial proposal was that we would have an eight-week period to opt out, starting from the moment that, like from that initial infra and like site build period.
The challenge there is that we wouldn't be able to start tracking or like measuring results until we start publishing content, so past the eight-week period.
So my suggestion is that we start the eight-week period from the moment we start publishing versus the moment we start building.
Got it.
And I mean, it's a brand new site and these...
would be the first articles we publish to that new site.
So what could we like reasonably expect to see eight weeks after those first articles are published?
Yeah.
So that's where the benchmark Matt was referring would come in.
Yeah.
And that's also why I think we should go for a much higher volume of content on this two-week period.
Like, yeah, and again, right, obviously, I understand that this is still fairly ambiguous.
There's no real numbers here to talk about.
And so I understand why it may not be helpful.
But as far as we're concerned, like, any traction would be an indicator that it could be successful and that we would want to continue.
So it's, you know, it's not like, you know, our expectations are not so overblown that we expect, like, oh, this is going to drive.
Let's look
Referral traffic to RAMP.
This is going to generate actually attributable revenue for RAMP.
That's not really what we're looking to accomplish within four to eight weeks.
It's like, how quickly can we get sighted?
At what rate are we being sighted?
If we can see any of those things, then that would be an indication that scaling this would continue to be beneficial.
But yes, again, it makes sense.
We should come to a conclusion on our side around what success would look like to use as some sort of threshold.
So yeah, I get that.
And I think what you explained is super helpful.
Like, that lets us know that it's not unrealistic expectations, right?
It's like seeing the vision and that there's progress and that we're going to continue to build on that.
I guess, like, since we're willing to, like, we really want to continue working on RAMP.com and we're willing to include that.
That complimentary, like who should we talk to, or who should you guys talk to, to figure out where we can fit in there?
Because there's got to be somewhere, right?
And I think if that's a part of it, then it de-risks the engagement, because we're not completely tied to an experiment, right?
We're tied to work that we know we can be successful around, and an experiment which we have a high degree of confidence will be successful.
Yeah.
I mean, to go back to what Victoria had been saying, know, the, kind of like a lot of the run rate content production that we have been working on is just kind of like no longer a focus for us.
We've, like, we've, reoriented most of the team to be-
Focused on AEO, which we split into two streams.
You know, that's owned AEO, and then there's earned AEO.
And so we would consider the cash flow desk to be earned AEO.
It is infinitely more scalable than dropping a shitload of money on affiliate sites for placements on random lists.
You know, I'm sure you can understand why, like, the idea behind the cash flow desk driving earned AEO is a fairly big bet for us.
And, like, that is where we really see the alpha in our AEO strategy going forward.
We are pretty much, like, with Sophie coming in on Monday, we're, like, staffed internally to be able to manage, essentially, the whole owned SEO channel.
Because with Sophie on board now, that'll kind of cover our bases from all of our content needs, as well as some, you know, automations that we've built internally.
Well, so, you know, if one of the sticking points is going to be, like, continuing work on RAMP.com, like, we would need to, we would be, like, forcing it to try to figure out where to slot in.
Does that feel fair, Victoria?
Does that sound accurate?
It does, yeah.
Yeah, and I guess that's why we were willing to make it more experimental on RAMP.com as well.
Like, maybe it's, you know, creating tools or micro apps or calculators.

And it seems like, you know, maybe there just needs to be a conversation between, like, Marcel and the decision maker around where there could be additional benefit for the work that we do on RAMP.com.
And I think what we can do on our side is we can definitely take what you're saying about, you know.
Having like a, it's like a seven week period out of the eight weeks in the strategy sprint where you could opt out.
And typically, Victoria, like that's a new client versus an existing client.
So we'll talk to Marcel about that and see how he feels.
But how can we also keep the conversation going on where GrowthX might be able to fit in on RAMP?
One other thing to note also would be that during that sprint phase, obviously the team will be working on AEO in particular.

And so there might be an opportunity within those few weeks to come up with us proposing some type of plan that we think could be beneficial to RAMP just with like a little bit more time to understand what strategies you're currently deploying and what your area's focus are.
So that's one of the things.
That could come out of the sprint.
So just want to note that that might be one avenue to explore.
And then agree with you, Tyler, where it might be helpful for like Marcel and George to say hello and kind of understand if there are other opportunities.
But I hear you, Matt, Victoria, that like right now it would be a force.
And obviously, you know, we can help with restructuring any of the articles, refreshing any of the articles.
Anyway, there are a host of other things that we could do, but I hear you on that.
But yeah, if there is stone left unturned, would love to hear.

That's a great point, Mara, because Victoria and Matt, for context, a lot of times in the strategy sprint with a new client, we'll identify lanes that we haven't identified in the sales conversation where there are work streams that GrowthX can execute on that would have value.
So we can do that work right in the first.
Eight weeks of the engagement for the cashflow desk.
And it's like, we're going to work on the cashflow desk, but we can also do the exact same work we do in a strategy sprint to identify what the next lane on RAMP.com could be.
I think, you know, what I would try to do on our side is give, you know, see if Marcel would be open to having the same opt-out window that we give a new client, right?

Which is like the first seven weeks of that engagement in this scenario so that, you know, if we can't identify another lane on RAMP and if you don't feel confident in the strategy that we're executing on for the cashflow desk, then you have that ability, right, to switch gears.
Do we think that could be like a viable path forward?
I don't think I, I don't think I understood, uh, where the seven week period would, uh, so you, your question, Victoria, is so that we can start the validation window from.

The moment that we start publishing versus the build sprint, because you're acknowledging that, like, it might be a little fast for us to do it just within that typical window during the sprint phase.
So I think, Tyler, correct me if I'm wrong, we need to confirm that with Marcel.
Also, I think what would be helpful is the benchmarks, if you are able to share any, just to, like, help de-risk it a little bit for us.
We can maybe write it in where it's, like, if we start seeing performance in this way, as you mentioned, Matt, like, we're comfortable kind of, like, auto-renewing.
And then during that sprint phase, also, we'll have the opportunity to, like, dig in and see if there are other AEO projects we would recommend for RAMP.com.
And then that might be how that part of the work looks.
Understanding that that is still, or as yet, unclear.
And also something that, obviously, we're doing without extending budget.
So it's, like, it's...
It's just kind of a bonus, but because that's something that we would really like to be involved in.
Does that sound like a good wrap up?
And the action items for us would be let's confirm with Marcel on the validation window.
And then RAMP Team, if you're able to share benchmarks, we can spin up a contract.
Maybe that makes everyone happy.
then ideally, maybe George and Marcel can catch up just so that they can catch up with one another.
But does that sound like a good way of thinking about it?
Yes, it does.
I think, yeah, as a first step, if you can share the validation window, I think that's a really important point for us, being able to start validation from the moment we start publishing.
And we'll, yeah, we can definitely, definitely get more structured benchmarks and like expect.
we'll a good work.
that's
Patience to you guys, so yeah, so we don't risk this, the period, yeah.
Cool.
Unfortunately, I don't, I can't give you like a really firm timeline on when we'll be able to deliver those because we're dependent on a third party to deliver the data to us.
Um, but like he, he knows that this is something that we want, uh, and he's known for a while.
So like, it's, it's on us to continue to, to put pressure to, to get the data.
Um, yeah, but once we have that, um, I'm hopeful that'll give us like some sort of proxy, uh, that we can use as a, as a benchmark.
Um, because it was a site started from scratch.
I think the only difference is that it's on a .org domain instead of .com, which might have some impact, but, um.
Yeah.
Yeah, as far as connecting Marcel with George, I mean, I don't see any issue with that full transparency.
Like when we discussed the engagement with George, the 30 day opt out came from him.
So like this, this is a, you know, there are a number of other vendors that we work with at RAMP.
And that's like built into the contract.
So, you know, I don't, I wouldn't say it's an expectation on his part, but like that was a suggestion from him.
So you, just so you know that it's not kind of coming from nowhere.
But yeah, I think, Victoria, think we can probably share some context with George and talk to him about getting him in touch with Marcel.
I think that would, I think that would help a lot just because like we talked to Marcel specifically about that.
Ask on the opt-out, and, you know, it was a pretty firm no just based on how much work we've already done together.

So I think if Marcel and George can discuss that point in addition to how flexible and creative we're willing to get with RAMP.com, it could allow us to get a proposal to you guys, not drag this out any longer than it needs to be dragged out, and, you know, we'll be able to have the terms that both sides agree to so that we head into that strategy sprint, right, like with confidence as opposed to, you know, uncertainty.
Yeah, I apologize.
I know we are already over, but I do have one more question just so I can kind of share some context with George.
George, we, it sounds like one of the sticking points is continuing work on the RAMP.com domain.
Is my understanding correct that you want for that?
Is to be included in the contract because it has been a proven source of performance?
Or what is driving the commitment to continuing to work for RAMP.com instead of off-site domains?
I think, you know, like, candidly, right, you know, you guys work with us, you guys work with AirOps, you guys work with Profound.

And since we've created a lot of pages that have been high-performing on RAMP, and since those are, that's obviously a great story for us to be able to tell, and one that a lot of times questions come up around, right?
Like, there's case studies with AirOps, there's case studies with Profound.

Like, what is GrowthX really doing for RAMP if we were to stop working on RAMP.com and, you know, only work on basically like an experiment for you guys, you know, it'd be bad for us, you know, from a business perspective, right?
Right, and being a standard.

It is possible here, like, since we've been able to be successful, like, we want to continue to drive results, and we've added work streams, like, we've added new capabilities, like, these tools could be a really interesting use case, or, you know, it can really be shaped in whatever way you guys want it to be.
So I think, right, like, if we're only doing this experiment, the relationship's much more fragile than it was before.
Hopefully I'm not being too honest there.
I don't think so.
No, get it.
Yep.
But I think it's, like, if we weren't driving results for you guys on RAMP, and if we didn't do great work, then we wouldn't be fighting so hard to keep being able to do it.
Just one thing I want to reinforce and add is that we spent a lot of time prioritizing our resources and, like, understanding where we should be focusing on, and where we...
We think the return will come from and we prioritized this versus a lot of work on RAMP.com.
So we really believe that this will drive return and that this will become something much bigger than just one site.
So this is not us throwing something random at you guys.
This is something we believe that will work and that we trust you guys to execute.
So, yeah.
Also, I just want to jump in and say thank you so much for like hearing, especially that you're breaking this up into two different pieces.
This would be one of those pieces and that you're kind of bringing us along.
Like, thank you very much for bringing us the opportunity and the idea and for being such a good partner throughout like these kind of conversations.
I know it's taken longer than we expected.
So thank you very much for like thinking of us, trusting us, and for like.
Having these conversations with us, like, we really appreciate it.
I really appreciate it, so.
A hundred percent.
Yeah, same here.
Like, putting myself in your shoes, like, I would even be maybe a little frustrated by how long this has taken.
And so that's why I think, like, you know, let's just connect Marcel and George specifically around the opt-out, specifically around if there's anything on RAMP.com.
Because right now it's like, we're having this conversation, and then Mara and I go to talk to Marcel, right?
You and Matt go talk to George.
And if we can just have the two meet, then we can get this agreement in place and start executing on the cash flow desk, which we so appreciate you giving us the opportunity to do, Victoria.
Like, we're excited about that project, absolutely.
We just want to make sure, like, there's a path to continue working together.
And, you know, right now it's basically just you and Matt fighting for that.
And maybe there's an opportunity to build a stronger relationship between Marcel and George.
Cool.
Okay.
Great.
I know we're quite over.
So any other questions or anything?
Otherwise, we can let you guys go.
We'll have Marcel and George connect.
And then we know that the whole benchmarks aspect, third party.
So hopefully, keep us updated whenever that can come.
And also, if you need anything else from us, please ping us and we'll do it on as short of a timeline as possible.
Yeah.
And we, and we probably don't even need that to tell you the truth to get a proposal prepared.
We just need the alignment between Marcel and George to create a proposal and send it to you.
Okay.
Okay.
Great.
Sounds good.
All right.
Thanks so much for your time.
Really appreciate it.
Thank you guys.
Thanks.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize terms for the Cashflow Desk project.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Validation Window:** The 8-week validation period will start at content publication, not site build, to allow for performance measurement.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [**Strategic Alignment:** The Cashflow Desk is RAMP's key "Earned AEO" bet for scalable link building, while the "Owned AEO" channel is now staffed internally.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [**Contract Structure:** The proposal will combine the Cashflow Desk with a flexible RAMP.com workstream (e.g., tools/calculators) to de-risk the engagement for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1315.0)
  - [**Path to Agreement:** Marcel (GrowthX) and George (RAMP) will connect to align on the opt-out clause and the RAMP.com workstream, resolving the main blockers.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=2168.0)

## Topics

### Vendor Page Strategy Shift

  - [Vendor page work is paused for a new design launch in \~2 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [**New Design:** More data-driven, less content-heavy, with a 5-bullet summary for social sharing.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot:** Testing a shift from an SEO play to a data product.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=359.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Driven by a different RAMP department leveraging new data resources.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=443.0)
  - [**Future Role for GrowthX:** Uncertain.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=340.0)
      - [**Contingency:** If RAMP reverts to the original design, GrowthX support would be needed.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=340.0)

### Cashflow Desk Project: Contract Terms

  - [**RAMP's Concern:** The 8-week validation period in the proposal starts at the site build, preventing performance validation before a year-long commitment.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
  - [**RAMP's Proposal:** Start the 8-week validation period at content publication.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1048.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This aligns the validation window with the ability to measure results.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Concern:** A 30-day opt-out is too ambiguous for a project without defined KPIs.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=982.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Success metrics (e.g., citation rate) are undefined, making it impossible to know what performance is required to avoid an opt-out.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=982.0)

### Strategic Alignment & Path Forward

  - [**RAMP's Strategic Focus:**](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
      - [**Earned AEO (Cashflow Desk):** The primary focus, seen as a scalable alternative to expensive affiliate placements.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
      - [**Owned AEO (RAMP.com):** Now fully staffed internally, reducing the need for external support on traditional content.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Position:**](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1959.0)
      - [**Rationale for RAMP.com Work:** Continuing work on the main domain is critical for business continuity and case study development.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1959.0)
      - [**Proposed Solution:** Add a flexible RAMP.com workstream (e.g., tools, calculators) to de-risk the engagement, as it's tied to a proven asset.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1315.0)
  - [**Agreed Path Forward:**](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1747.0)
      - [**Validation Window:** GrowthX will confirm with Marcel if the 8-week period can start at publication.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1749.0)
      - [**Benchmarks:** RAMP will provide citation rate data from a proxy site ("new facts") to define success metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [**Executive Alignment:** Marcel (GrowthX) and George (RAMP) will connect to resolve the opt-out clause and define the RAMP.com workstream.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=2168.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1749.0)
      - [Confirm with Marcel if the 8-week validation window can start at content publication.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1749.0)
      - [Connect Marcel and George to align on contract terms.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=2168.0)
  - [**RAMP:**](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1749.0)
      - [Provide citation rate benchmarks from the "new facts" site to define success metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [Brief George on the discussion to prepare for the call with Marcel.](https://fathom.video/share/qWsYmfxPdMuPA1Mhb7adLDWvrmpyf7sh?tab=summary&timestamp=1959.0)

action_items.description: Confirm w/ Marcel validation window starts at publish; then send terms to Victoria/Matt
Intro Marcel <> George re: opt-out + RAMP.com; then GrowthX send proposal to Victoria/Matt
Share benchmarks w/ GrowthX once 3rd-party data arrives
